title: my parents want to take out a loan for $7,000 CAD for “alternative cancer treatment” for my mum with stage iv breast cancer. i’m concerned that it might be a scam
body:
i have no one else to go to but i really need advice because i feel like i’m losing my mind.

my mum (51f) was recently diagnosed with stage iv breast cancer that metastasised to other parts of her body. she is in constant pain, mostly bedbound, and needs help even showering.

instead of consulting an oncologist properly, my father (71m) is considering an unverified overseas “treatment” that a man in canada is trying to sell them. he claims he has cured 199 out of 200 cancer patients using a mix of drugs which i can’t list because of the subreddit rules but i looked all of them up and none of them seem like legitimate treatments for cancer (which i expected).

this man sent us a “treatment plan” costing $7,000 CAD (we are not wealthy and would have to take out a loan). by the way, we live all the way in jamaica so it’s even more suspicious that this “doctor” is able to treat people without even having face to face consultations or anything.

there are no credentials provided, no published data, no proof of his claimed cure rate, and no independent verification. just word of mouth.

my dad says we should take out a loan because this is our “best chance.” i think this is a scam and that we’re being targeted because we’re desperate. as you can tell, my parents are up in age and may be unable to see the signs of what is obviously a dishonest scheme that targets vulnerable people who are in search of hope in a situation that is otherwise hopeless. i (17f), am a more practical person and i try to think with logic as opposed to emotion. that money could be spent on treatment that may actually provide a fighting chance and it just sucks that they aren’t even willing to listen to reason.

i’m terrified that:

	1.	this will financially ruin us

	2.	my mum will lose valuable time without proper medical care

	3.	these medications could actually harm her

i’ve tried reasoning calmly but they think that actual doctors “only give bad news” and that this alternative doctor has better success.

i don’t know how to get through to them. has anyone dealt with something like this? how do you stop someone from making a massive financial and medical mistake when they’re operating from fear?

please be honest. i just need grounded advice.
